name: Kaiser Marshall
abstract: Joseph "Kaiser" Marshall (June 11, 1902 in Savannah, Georgia – January 2, 1948 in New York City) was an American jazz drummer. Marshall was raised in Boston, where he studied under George L.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Armstrong
about_quotes.evidence: even when I joined Fletcher Henderson at the Roseland Ballroom I was just 24 years old and in that band you had Colvin Hawkins, Don Redmond, Kaiser Marshall, Fletcher at the piano...

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I saw him, and I introduced myself to him -- this was Kaiser Marshall... So I went to the Rhythm Club and I met another guy... I met Kaiser Marshall

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: In Camp Unity... I had Sandy Williams, Kaiser Marshall, Cecil Scott, Herb Ward, Henry Goodwin and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with Fletcher's band... we had guys in the band like... Kaiser Marshall

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, Johnny Russell, myself, Glyn Paque, me, Bob McRae and Kaiser Marshall.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Blumberg
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee corrects the interviewer regarding Kaiser Marshall, stating 'Not Kaiser' and later confirms Marshall was with the band 'one time' in the context of Bunk Johnson's troubles, but shows no personal interaction or relationship with Marshall himself.
